articles.title: Designing Net-Zero Homes on Urban Infill Lots
articles.excerpt: How to achieve net-zero energy performance when building modern homes on tight city lots.
articles.content:
Building a net-zero home on an urban infill lot requires careful coordination of site planning, envelope design, and building systems. Compact forms with high-performance envelopes minimize heat loss, while generous glazing is balanced with exterior shading and low-solar-gain glass. Roof space is reserved for a right-sized photovoltaic array, and mechanical systems prioritize all-electric solutions such as heat pumps and heat-recovery ventilation.

Because infill sites are often constrained, early massing studies help optimize solar orientation and privacy. Detailing at party walls and shared property lines must also account for acoustics and fire separation. We frequently integrate green roofs and high-albedo surfaces to mitigate urban heat island effects and improve stormwater management.

From the outset, we model energy performance to understand trade-offs between envelope upgrades and mechanical system complexity. This allows clients to make informed decisions about where to invest for the greatest impact on both comfort and long-term operating costs.

articles.title: Green Kitchen Renovations: Materials That Matter
articles.excerpt: A practical guide to choosing sustainable materials, finishes, and appliances for your next kitchen remodel.
articles.content:
Kitchen renovations offer one of the best opportunities to reduce the environmental footprint of your home. Durable, low-emission materials improve indoor air quality while reducing long-term replacement cycles. We prioritize cabinet boxes made from FSC-certified plywood with no-added-formaldehyde adhesives, paired with low-VOC finishes.

Countertop choices such as recycled-content quartz or solid-surface products with Environmental Product Declarations give clients transparency into embodied carbon. For flooring, we often specify sustainably harvested wood or high-quality linoleum rather than vinyl. Efficient LED task lighting and induction cooktops significantly cut energy use and improve comfort in the space.

Appliance selection is another key driver of performance. ENERGY STAR-rated dishwashers and refrigerators, combined with well-designed ventilation, support both energy efficiency and occupant health. By making a series of thoughtful, incremental decisions, a typical kitchen renovation can become a model of sustainability without sacrificing aesthetics.

articles.title: Sustainable Multifamily Design Trends for 2023
articles.excerpt: From low-carbon structures to shared amenities, multifamily housing is leading the next wave of green building.
articles.content:
Multifamily projects are uniquely positioned to advance sustainable design because they aggregate demand for high-performance systems and shared amenities. In 2023, we see a strong push toward mass timber structures, which significantly reduce embodied carbon compared to conventional concrete and steel.

Passive solar strategies, operable shading, and natural ventilation are being integrated with high-efficiency central mechanical systems. Amenity spaces such as rooftop gardens and shared work lounges not only build community but also support stormwater management and improved thermal comfort.

EV-ready parking, secure bike storage, and thoughtfully designed pedestrian connections further reduce transportation-related emissions. As energy codes tighten, we expect an increased emphasis on post-occupancy monitoring, allowing building operators to fine-tune systems and ensure that design intent is realized in day-to-day performance.

articles.title: Choosing the Right House Plan for a Narrow City Lot
articles.excerpt: Key considerations when selecting a pre-designed home plan for tight urban sites.
articles.content:
Narrow city lots demand house plans that make every foot count. Vertical stacking, carefully aligned plumbing walls, and strategic window placement help smaller footprints feel generous. When evaluating pre-designed plans, pay attention to circulation patterns and how rooms borrow light from each other.

We recommend identifying which spaces must have exterior windows and which can rely on clerestories or light wells. Storage solutions built into stair runs and underutilized corners keep clutter at bay. Finally, consider how your plan will adapt over time—flex rooms that can shift from home office to guest space will extend the usefulness of a compact home.

articles.title: Cost Planning for Custom Homes in 2024
articles.excerpt: Understand the key drivers of construction cost before you design your custom home.
articles.content:
Construction costs continue to evolve, driven by labor availability, material pricing, and regulatory requirements. In 2024, we encourage clients to establish a realistic budget early and maintain a contingency of at least 10–15%. Site conditions, structural complexity, and level of finish all have a measurable impact on overall cost.

Our cost calculator provides a starting point based on square footage, location, and finish level. During schematic design, we refine these numbers with input from preferred builders. Clear communication between owner, architect, and contractor helps prevent scope creep and minimizes surprises during bidding.

articles.title: Modern Farmhouse vs Contemporary: Whats the Difference?
articles.excerpt: Clarifying the distinctions between two popular residential styles.
articles.content:
Modern farmhouse and contemporary homes often get lumped together, but they emphasize different design priorities. Modern farmhouse draws from traditional rural forms, featuring gable roofs, lap siding, and warm, tactile materials. Contemporary design focuses on clean lines, flat or low-slope roofs, and larger expanses of glass.

Inside, modern farmhouse aesthetics lean toward exposed beams, shiplap, and layered textures, while contemporary interiors are more minimal and sculptural. Both styles can be executed with high performance in mind, but the architectural language and detailing will feel distinct.

articles.title: Navigating Permits for Urban Infill Projects
articles.excerpt: Why entitlements and permits are often the longest phase of an urban infill project.
articles.content:
Urban infill projects frequently require variances, design review, and coordination with multiple agencies. Understanding zoning overlays, height limits, and parking requirements is essential before committing to a site. Early in our process, we perform a zoning and code audit to identify potential hurdles.

Community engagement is another critical factor. Many cities encourage neighborhood input for infill projects, and a thoughtful outreach plan can build trust and support. Well-prepared permit sets and proactive responses to plan check comments help keep the schedule on track.

articles.title: Lighting Design Basics for Cozy Living Spaces
articles.excerpt: Layered lighting can transform the way your home feels at different times of day.
articles.content:
Effective lighting design balances ambient, task, and accent lighting to create flexible, inviting rooms. In living spaces, we pair dimmable recessed fixtures with floor and table lamps to avoid glare and harsh shadows. Warm color temperatures (2700K400K) support relaxation, while task lighting near seating and reading areas ensures comfort.

Integrating lighting controls into your design from the start helps avoid wall clutter and provides intuitive scenes for different activities. We also pay close attention to daylighting, ensuring that artificial lighting complements natural light rather than competing with it.

articles.title: High-Performance Building Envelopes in Mild Climates
articles.excerpt: Why envelope performance still matters even when winters are relatively mild.
articles.content:
In mild climates, it can be tempting to downplay envelope performance, but airtightness and insulation still drive comfort and energy efficiency. Thermal comfort is about more than air temperaturesurface temperatures and drafts play a significant role. We focus on continuous insulation, carefully detailed air barriers, and high-quality windows with appropriate solar heat gain coefficients.

Balanced ventilation with heat recovery improves indoor air quality year-round, while shading strategies limit overheating during increasingly common heat waves. These measures provide resilience as climate patterns shift and energy costs fluctuate.

articles.title: Designing for Aging in Place in Suburban Homes
articles.excerpt: Strategies to make todays remodels support tomorrows needs.
articles.content:
Aging in place design goes beyond grab bars. We look at circulation widths, level entries, and the potential for a main-floor bedroom and bath. Subtle design moves, such as reinforcing walls at future grab bar locations or pre-wiring for future stair lifts, can be built into current projects at minimal cost.

Thoughtful lighting, acoustics, and material selection all contribute to comfort and safety. By planning ahead, homeowners can remain in familiar neighborhoods and avoid disruptive relocations later in life.

services.name: Custom Home Design & Build Assistance
services.short_description: End-to-end architectural services for new custom homes, from concept through construction.
services.full_description: Our custom home design service guides you from initial vision through move-in. We begin with a detailed programming phase to understand how you live, then develop concepts that respond to your site, budget, and aesthetic preferences. Our team produces permit and construction drawings, coordinates consultants, and can assist with builder selection and construction observation. An integrated cost calculator helps align design decisions with your budget in real time.

services.name: Renovations & Remodels
services.short_description: Thoughtful renovations, additions, and interior transformations for existing homes.
services.full_description: From kitchen updates to whole-house remodels, our renovation team helps you reimagine your existing home. We focus on improving flow, daylight, and energy performance while respecting the character you love. Services include feasibility studies, phased master plans, permit drawings, and finish selections tailored to your budget and schedule.

services.name: Commercial Architecture
services.short_description: Design services for offices, retail, and mixed-use developments.
services.full_description: We partner with business owners and developers to create efficient, flexible commercial environmentsfrom adaptive reuse of existing structures to ground-up office, retail, and mixed-use buildings. Our work balances brand expression, occupant comfort, and long-term operational efficiency.

services.name: Interior Design & Styling
services.short_description: Coordinated interiors, furnishings, and lighting tailored to your architecture.
services.full_description: Our interior design team develops cohesive palettes of finishes, fixtures, and furnishings to bring your spaces to life. We coordinate millwork, lighting, and furniture layouts with the architectural plan, ensuring that every element supports comfort, durability, and your personal style.

services.name: Architecture Consulting Only
services.short_description: Targeted consultations for feasibility, budgeting, or design direction.
services.full_description: For clients who are not yet ready to commit to a full design scope, we offer consulting-only engagements. These may include site and zoning reviews, high-level test fits, second-opinion plan reviews, or guidance on how to phase a project over time. Sessions can be conducted in-studio or virtually.

testimonials.summary: Outstanding communication kept our narrow-lot urban infill home on schedule.
testimonials.full_text:
Building a tall, narrow home on a tiny infill lot in the Pearl District sounded stressful, but the team turned it into a surprisingly smooth experience. From the first meeting, they set clear expectations around budget and schedule, and then backed that up with consistent communication.

We received regular check-ins at each design milestone and during construction, so we always knew what decisions were coming next and how they would affect the timeline and cost. When city review took longer than expected, they had already built in contingency time and explained exactly how our timeline would shift.

The finished home takes full advantage of the lot with a roof deck, great privacy from neighbors, and an elevator-ready core that will help us age in place. We never felt out of the loop, and that level of communication made a complex urban infill project feel manageable.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “We never felt out of the loop—their communication kept our urban infill project on schedule and on budget.”

testimonials.summary: Our Maple Street home feels twice as large thanks to smart planning and daylight.
testimonials.full_text:
We came to the firm with a straightforward wish list: three bedrooms, lots of natural light, and a strong connection to our small yard. What we received was a Maple Street home that feels far larger than its square footage. The open-plan main level, carefully placed windows, and built-in storage make day-to-day living incredibly comfortable.

They were attentive listeners and translated our vague ideas—like “a place to drop bags that doesn’t become a mess”—into specific design moves. The mudroom, pantry, and window seats all work hard for our family.

Even our builder commented on how well-organized the drawings were, which made construction efficient and reduced surprises. We feel like we got a custom-level design within our target budget.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “The house feels twice as large as it is because every square foot was thoughtfully planned.”

testimonials.summary: They gave us clear timeline expectations for a complex stepped foundation and courtyard design.
testimonials.full_text:
Our steep city lot had scared off other designers, but this team immediately saw potential in the hillside. They proposed a stepped residence organized around a sheltered courtyard, and then carefully explained what that meant for structure, drainage, and schedule.

From the outset they laid out a realistic timeline that accounted for survey work, engineering, and an extended permitting process due to the slope. As we moved through each phase, they updated the timeline and flagged critical decision points so that we didn’t inadvertently cause delays.

The courtyard now feels like an outdoor living room, protected from street noise and wind, and the house flows effortlessly down the site. Their honest conversations about timeline and trade-offs helped us make decisions with confidence.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “They were upfront about the timeline from day one, which kept a tricky hillside project surprisingly calm.”

testimonials.summary: Our farmhouse feels timeless and practical, with spaces that grow with our kids.
testimonials.full_text:
We wanted a farmhouse that felt timeless, not trendy, and that would grow with our young kids. The Cedar Lane design balances those goals beautifully. The wrap-around porch is everyone’s favorite spot, and the open kitchen and dining area have become the heart of the home.

The design team was particularly thoughtful about how rooms relate to each other—sight lines from the kitchen to the play area, storage near the entry, and a quiet corner office tucked away from the noise. They also helped us prioritize where to invest in higher-end finishes and where we could save without compromising the overall feel.

Neighbors constantly comment on how well the house fits the wooded lot. It looks like it has always been there, in the best possible way.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “They gave us a farmhouse that feels both timeless and perfectly tailored to our family.”

testimonials.summary: Our 2022 kitchen renovation maximized storage, light, and sustainable finishes within our budget.
testimonials.full_text:
Our original kitchen was dark, cramped, and cut off from the rest of the house. Within a relatively modest budget, the team completely transformed it. By reworking a few key walls and rethinking circulation, they created a bright, efficient kitchen that feels connected to the dining and living spaces.

We appreciated their guidance on sustainable materials—low-VOC finishes, durable cabinet boxes, and efficient lighting—and how they clearly labeled “good, better, best” options so we could make informed choices. The result feels warm and inviting, not sterile.

Even months later, we still notice small details that make daily routines easier, like the landing space near the fridge and the way the pantry is tucked out of view but easy to access.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “They turned a dark, cramped kitchen into the most joyful room in our house—without blowing our budget.”

testimonials.summary: They transformed a tiny loft bathroom into a spa-like retreat with clever storage.
testimonials.full_text:
My loft bathroom started as a purely utilitarian space—small, awkward, and lacking any sense of calm. The team rethought every inch, adding a walk-in shower, custom vanity, and just the right amount of built-in storage.

They paid close attention to finishes and lighting, suggesting materials that felt luxurious but were still practical for a compact city bathroom. The new layout makes the room feel twice as large, even though the footprint barely changed.

From design through construction, they coordinated smoothly with my contractor and quickly answered questions that came up on site. The result is a daily retreat that genuinely improves my quality of life.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “It feels like a boutique hotel bathroom, somehow fit into my tiny loft.”

testimonials.summary: For a high-budget urban infill office conversion, their communication and timeline management were exceptional.
testimonials.full_text:
Converting an aging warehouse into modern creative offices in the downtown core was a high-stakes investment for our partnership. We needed a design team that could balance bold ideas with the realities of code upgrades, seismic work, and a tight urban site.

They established a clear communication structure from day one: weekly check-ins, shared tracking of decisions, and a single point of contact for questions. That level of organization was critical as we navigated historic review, structural upgrades, and changing tenant requirements.

The project carried a significant budget, and they were transparent about how design choices affected both cost and schedule. When supply chain issues emerged mid-construction, they worked with the contractor to adjust the phasing and sequence so the overall timeline remained realistic.

Our completed office now leases easily, tenants love the daylight and exposed structure, and the building has become a showcase project for our portfolio.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “On a complex, multi-million-dollar urban infill project, their communication and control of the timeline gave us real confidence.”

testimonials.summary: Patients comment on how bright and calming the new clinic feels.
testimonials.full_text:
As a medical practice, we needed a clinic that was efficient for staff, accessible for patients, and calming rather than clinical. The design team approached the project by shadowing our staff and mapping daily flows, which led to a layout that minimizes backtracking and bottlenecks.

They prioritized daylight in exam rooms and waiting areas while carefully considering privacy. Wayfinding is intuitive, with clear sight lines and subtle color cues that help patients navigate without feeling overwhelmed.

Throughout the process they also coordinated well with our equipment vendors and the general contractor, helping ensure that specialized requirements were met without compromising the overall design.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “The new clinic runs more smoothly, and patients constantly mention how bright and calming it feels.”

testimonials.summary: They balanced density, privacy, and shared outdoor space in a tight urban infill townhome project.
testimonials.full_text:
Our goal for Garden Court Townhomes was to add gentle density to a walkable neighborhood without overwhelming it. The design team responded with a courtyard-driven plan that gives each home good light and privacy while still offering a sense of community.

They were attentive to neighbors’ concerns during the entitlement process and adjusted the massing and landscaping strategy to reduce perceived height and overlook. Inside, the homes feel generous despite compact footprints, which has translated into strong buyer interest and quick sales.

Their experience with multifamily work was evident in the way they handled code issues, egress, and shared services. The project has been a financial and community success.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “The courtyard concept turned a tight infill site into a real community asset.”

testimonials.summary: They turned a dated high-rise condo into a warm, modern home tailored to my routines.
testimonials.full_text:
My high-rise condo had great views but felt cold and generic. The interior design team focused on how I actually live—where I read, work, and host friends—and reworked the plan and finishes around those routines.

They introduced warm wood tones, improved lighting, and a much smarter storage strategy that hides clutter but keeps essentials within reach. We didn’t move any structural walls, but the unit feels completely transformed.

The coordination with the building’s management and contractors was seamless, which made the renovation far less disruptive than I expected.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “It finally feels like a home designed for me, not just another unit in a tower.”

testimonials.summary: Our lakeside cabin feels cozy in winter and open to the outdoors in summer.
testimonials.full_text:
We dreamed of a small cabin that would function well for just the two of us but also expand to welcome family and friends. The design hits that balance beautifully. In the cooler months, the great room with its fireplace feels intimate and snug; in summer, wide doors open the space to the deck and lake beyond.

The team respected the rural character of the site, working around existing trees and shaping the roofline to sit comfortably in the landscape. They also helped us choose durable, low-maintenance exterior materials so we can spend more time relaxing and less time on upkeep.

It’s exactly the retreat we hoped for.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “The cabin feels like it grew out of the site—it’s simple, durable, and exactly what we needed.”

testimonials.summary: Flexible floor plates and shared amenities have made the building a magnet for startups.
testimonials.full_text:
For the Midtown Startup Hub, we needed a building that could evolve with changing tenants and workstyles. The design team focused on flexible floor plates, generous shared amenities, and straightforward building systems that are easy to adapt.

Large windows, exposed structure, and simple, durable finishes give the spaces character without locking tenants into a specific aesthetic. The central stair and common areas encourage chance encounters, which our tenants love.

Leasing has been strong, and brokers consistently cite the layout and natural light as major selling points.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “They gave us a building that’s easy to lease and even easier for tenants to make their own.”

testimonials.summary: Retail, housing, and streetscape improvements came together in a cohesive mixed-use block.
testimonials.full_text:
HarborPoint was our first ground-up mixed-use project, and we relied heavily on the design team to guide us through competing priorities. They balanced the needs of ground-floor retail, upper-level apartments, and the surrounding streetscape in a way that feels cohesive and inviting.

Retail tenants appreciate the high ceilings and flexible bays, while residents enjoy quiet units set back from the street with well-planned amenities. Thoughtful detailing at the base of the building has made the sidewalks feel safer and more active.

They also coordinated effectively with city staff and our civil engineer to address stormwater and access requirements without compromising the overall vision.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “They helped us deliver a mixed-use project that serves both our tenants and the surrounding neighborhood.”

testimonials.summary: Deep overhangs and terraces created a retail destination that works year-round.
testimonials.full_text:
For our Riverside Retail Pavilion, we wanted something more than a typical strip center. The design team proposed a pavilion-style building with deep overhangs, generous outdoor terraces, and strong visual connections to the adjacent riverfront.

Those design moves have paid off—tenants use the terraces for outdoor seating almost year-round, and the building has become a recognizable landmark along the corridor. The careful attention to sun angles and weather protection has kept operating costs reasonable and made the spaces comfortable in every season.

They were attentive to tenant feedback along the way and adjusted details to support signage, deliveries, and outdoor merchandising.
testimonials.highlight_quote: “Our tenants love the terraces, and customers recognize the building immediately.”
